epics.title: Align the songs workspace with the draft token baseline

tasks.title: Identify repeated songs-surface literals that should map to shared draft tokens
acceptance_criteria: The scoped work targets repeated color, spacing, and typography literals in the songs route and related components.
The task keeps existing layout behavior and selection flow intact.
The audit distinguishes between values that can bind directly to tokens now and values that should remain local.
done_definition: The design phase can reference a bounded set of token-alignment targets across the songs surface.

tasks.title: Replace practical songs-surface literals with draft token bindings while preserving the established aesthetic
acceptance_criteria: The songs page, workspace, list, and score viewer use shared token variables for the repeated warm palette and typography roles where practical.
The existing warm restrained editorial feel remains intact.
No user-facing interaction or information architecture changes are introduced.
done_definition: The songs surface relies less on one-off literals and more on shared token-backed values.

tasks.title: Validate the updated songs surface and record any residual mismatches
acceptance_criteria: Relevant checks pass for the changed files when applicable.
The run artifacts validate and remaining token-alignment gaps are recorded explicitly.
done_definition: The run can complete without hidden follow-up work inside the same scope.

assumptions: The songs workspace is the correct first product surface to normalize against the draft token baseline.
Arbitrary Tailwind values that reference CSS variables are an acceptable bridge before broader semantic utility extraction.
risks: Some tuned local values may still need to remain local if direct token replacement would reduce legibility.
Color normalization may expose slight inconsistencies between the draft token palette and the current tuned songs surface.
